keyPoints: Isomorphism: Graphs that are mapped to each other in a way that preserves edge connections
Sub-Graphs: Smaller graphs within a larger graph, formed by sharing edges and vertices
Walks: Sequence of vertices and edges that form a valid path in the graph
Paths: Special type of walk where we don't revisit any vertex
Circuits: Closed walks that start and end at the same vertex
Connected Graphs: Graphs where there is a path between every pair of vertices
Disconnected Graphs: Graphs with no path between certain pairs of vertices
Components: Maximum number of connected sub-graphs in a graph
